methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
ADASI, an EDGE Group–backed sovereign-funded frontier-tech company in Defense & Security, presents a valuation challenge at the intersection of classified development and public market comparables. The addressable peer universe spans 21 same-sector companies, with 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Standard peer-median triangulation is not applicable here. The analysis identified zero same-stage matches, zero adjacent-stage matches, and zero similar-tier datapoints within the narrow Defense & Security cohort—the threshold for statistical reliability is three or more. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the sector's distinctive risk, regulatory, and capex profiles relative to broader frontier tech. Consequently, conventional comparable-company valuation cannot be reliably anchored.

The constraints reflect ADASI's position as a sovereign-backed, likely early-to-mid stage program with classified or restricted technical scope. Without sufficient narrow-peer granularity, revenue multiples, margin profiles, or growth benchmarks cannot be defensibly derived. A credible valuation would require either (1) discounted cash flow modeling grounded in program-level cost and milestone data, (2) precedent transaction analysis in classified defense tech, or (3) expansion of the peer set through adjacent sectors. Until such data is available, quantitative range synthesis is not warranted.
